question.text: Which of the following is NOT an eligible investment/expenditure for deduction under Section 80C?

answer.text: Tuition fees for two children paid to a university in India
Health insurance premium paid for self and family
Principal repayment of a housing loan

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Health insurance premium is deductible under Section 80D, NOT Section 80C. Section 80C covers: PPF, ELSS, EPF, life insurance premium, NSC, tuition fees (for max 2 children), housing loan principal, tax-saving FD (5 years), SCSS, and Sukanya Samriddhi Account, among others. Students frequently confuse 80C (investment-linked) with 80D (health insurance). **The Trap:** All other options are valid 80C deductions. Health insurance feels like it could be under 80C because it is a common tax-saving expenditure, but it has its own dedicated section (80D). **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A MFD exam tests 80C eligible items extensively, particularly ELSS versus other 80C instruments. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Keep a mental checklist separating 80C (investments/savings) from 80D (health insurance). Life insurance premium goes under 80C, but health insurance premium goes under 80D — a distinction clients frequently miss.
